fuzzy_prd:
We need a library for working with the SAML2 single sign-on protocol at the level of well-typed domain objects. Integration teams working with identity providers and service providers currently hand-write fragile XML parsing, mishandle time zones in timestamps, and re-implement the URL encoding that carries protocol messages across the browser. We want one well-defined contract covering: a UTC timestamp value type that only accepts properly UTC-tagged instants (with one exception for the zero/uninitialized default), comparison of offset-aware timestamps against UTC ones; a name-identifier type whose equality rules match the SAML spec (two identifiers are equal only when both their value and their optional format agree, an absent format only matches an absent format); readers that parse authentication request XML, assertion XML, protocol response XML, entity-descriptor metadata XML, and identity-provider role descriptor XML into domain objects; and a redirect-binding decoder that recovers a message from a query-string URL. All structural failures should surface as stable named error categories — handle the error reporting the same way as the XML traversal layer does, never surfacing host-language runtime details or stack traces. The redirect binding uses raw-deflate compression and URL-safe base64. Metadata entity descriptors must validate that the root element is in the correct SAML namespace and that at least one role descriptor child is present.

One small clarification on the timestamp behavior since the team asked about the default case: the zero/default timestamp (ticks=0) is accepted when its kind marker is 'unspecified'. This represents the uninitialized default. All other unspecified-kind timestamps are rejected with error=non_utc_datetime_kind. 'local' kind is always rejected regardless of tick count.

Also wanted to pin down the stable error names so nobody has to guess later. For XML shape issues, use 'missing_attribute' when a required XML attribute is absent, 'missing_element' when a required child element is absent, 'unexpected_local_name' when an element has the wrong local name, and 'unexpected_namespace' when an element is in the wrong XML namespace. On the redirect side, use 'message_parameter_not_found' when there is no SAMLRequest or SAMLResponse, 'duplicate_message_parameters' when both SAMLRequest and SAMLResponse are present, and 'duplicate_relay_state' when RelayState appears more than once.

And on the browser redirect encoding, the XML is raw-deflate compressed (no zlib header, RFC 1951), then URL-safe base64 encoded. The query parameter is named exactly 'SAMLRequest' or 'SAMLResponse'. An optional 'RelayState' parameter carries metadata verbatim.

oracle_data.golden_api_signature: public class SamlUtcDateTime; public class NameId; public static AuthnRequest ReadAuthnRequest(XmlElement); public static Assertion ReadAssertion(XmlElement); public static Response ReadResponse(XmlElement); public static EntityDescriptor ReadEntityDescriptor(XmlElement); public static void DecodeRedirectBinding(Uri url, out string name, out string xml, out string relayState)

trigger_keywords: what does the operator equality do differently from the equals check for NameId
how does null null comparison work for the operator
what is the difference between equals and operator for name identifiers
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: The 'operator' equality (==) additionally: treats two null/absent identifiers as equal (result=true); treats any null identifier as unequal to any non-null identifier (even an identifier with empty value/format). The 'equals' structural check does not have this null-handling behavior.

trigger_keywords: what does the wantAuthnRequestsSigned field default to
what is the default value of wantAuthnRequestsSigned in the IDP descriptor
when the WantAuthnRequestsSigned attribute is absent what value is used
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: When the WantAuthnRequestsSigned attribute is absent from the IDPSSODescriptor, it defaults to false. Output: 'wantAuthnRequestsSigned=false'.

The adapter routes by top-level action. For redirect_decode, a URL with neither SAMLRequest nor SAMLResponse must output exactly error=message_parameter_not_found. Duplicate SAMLRequest/SAMLResponse outputs error=duplicate_message_parameters, and duplicate RelayState outputs error=duplicate_relay_state.

trigger_keywords: how should I organize the code
should I split into multiple files
should I separate business logic from IO
should business logic touch stdin stdout
how to separate parsing from logic
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: Scale-driven code organization: match physical structure to domain complexity. For micro-utilities or simple scripts a single well-organized file is fine. For systems with multiple distinct responsibilities (I/O routing, business rules, formatters, etc.) you MUST use a multi-file layout (e.g. src/, tests/) — never a single god file. Do not over-engineer simple problems, but strictly avoid monolithic files for complex domains. Strict separation of concerns is mandatory: core business logic must be completely decoupled from stdin/stdout and JSON parsing. The JSON test cases are a black-box contract for the execution adapter only — NOT the internal data model. Follow SOLID: SRP (separate parsing, routing, core execution, output formatting); OCP (core engine open for extension, closed for modification); DIP (high-level modules depend on abstractions, not I/O details). The public interface must be idiomatic. Errors must be modeled properly (typed exceptions or Result/Monad patterns), not generic faults.

trigger_keywords: how does the test harness call my code
what is the entry point for testing
how does the runner call the program
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: You must implement an execution adapter (dispatcher) that is logically and ideally physically separate from the core domain. It reads a single JSON object from stdin, inspects the 'action' field to route to the correct core function, then prints the result to stdout matching the per-feature output contract. All policy durations in inputs are in seconds; all time points are epoch milliseconds. The adapter must not contain any business logic — every numeric result and boolean decision is delegated to the core domain.

Deliver a bash test harness at rcb_tests/test.sh. It must accept --cases-dir <subdir> (default: test_cases) to point at a directory of *.json case files under rcb_tests/. For each case file it runs every case in the 'cases' array against the dispatcher, writing raw stdout (no PASS/FAIL metadata) to: rcb_tests/stdout/<cases-dir>/{filename_stem}@{case_index_zero_padded_3}.txt Example: rcb_tests/stdout/public_test_cases/feature1_next_retry_at@000.txt Output is namespaced by <cases-dir> so different directories never overwrite each other. A single entry point 'bash rcb_tests/test.sh' runs the full suite with the default dir.

trigger_keywords: can I see the test inputs
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: Yes, public test cases are provided, one file per feature. Ask me for a specific feature by name and I will give you the full test case JSON.

context_pointers.vague_reference: handle the error reporting the same way as the XML traversal layer does
context_pointers.actual_meaning: All errors are reported as 'error=<category_name>' on a single line with no additional details. The category names are stable identifiers defined in the XmlTraverser class in src/Sustainsys.Saml2/Xml/XmlTraverser.cs.

oracle_data.fallback_response: I don't have specific information about that detail. Please refer to the start.md specification and the public test cases in rcb_tests/public_test_cases/ for authoritative requirements.

feature1_1_timestamp_kind_validation.jso.description: Convert wall-clock timestamp tagged with time-zone-kind to UTC. Only 'utc' and zero-ticks 'unspecified' are accepted.

feature1_2_timestamp_comparison.json.description: Compare offset-aware timestamp against UTC timestamp.

feature2_1_nameid_equality.json.description: Name identifier equality: both value and format must agree.

cases.expected_output:
op=equals
left.value=https://idp1.example.com
left.format=
right.value=https://idp1.example.com
right.format=
result=true

op=equals
left.value=https://idp1.example.com
left.format=
right.value=https://idp2.example.com
right.format=
result=false

feature2_2_nameid_string_equality.json.description: Name identifier equals bare string only when no format set.

feature3_authn_request_read.json.description: Parse SAML2 AuthnRequest XML. Missing Version yields missing_attribute error.

feature4_assertion_read.json.description: Parse SAML2 Assertion XML with structural validation.

feature8_redirect_binding_decode.json.description: Decode SAML2 redirect binding URL. SAMLRequest or SAMLResponse with raw-deflate + base64.
